00030 #include <types.h>
00031 #include <kern/errno.h>
00032 #include <kern/reboot.h>
00033 #include <kern/unistd.h>
00034 #include <limits.h>
00035 #include <lib.h>
00036 #include <uio.h>
00037 #include <clock.h>
00038 #include <thread.h>
00039 #include <vfs.h>
00040 #include <sfs.h>
00041 #include <syscall.h>
00042 #include <test.h>
00043 #include "opt-synchprobs.h"
00044 #include "opt-sfs.h"
00045 #include "opt-net.h"
00046 
00047 /*
00048  * In-kernel menu and command dispatcher.
00049  */
00050 
00051 #define _PATH_SHELL "/bin/sh"
00052 
00053 #define MAXMENUARGS  16
00054 
00055 // XXX this should not be in this file
00056 void
00057 getinterval(time_t s1, uint32_t ns1, time_t s2, uint32_t ns2,
00058             time_t *rs, uint32_t *rns)
00059 {
00060         if (ns2 < ns1) {
00061                 ns2 += 1000000000;
00062                 s2--;
00063         }
00064 
00065         *rns = ns2 - ns1;
00066         *rs = s2 - s1;
00067 }
00068 
00070 //
00071 // Command menu functions 
00072 
00073 /*
00074  * Function for a thread that runs an arbitrary userlevel program by
00075  * name.
00076  *
00077  * Note: this cannot pass arguments to the program. You may wish to 
00078  * change it so it can, because that will make testing much easier
00079  * in the future.
00080  *
00081  * It copies the program name because runprogram destroys the copy
00082  * it gets by passing it to vfs_open(). 
00083  */
00084 static
00085 void
00086 cmd_progthread(void *ptr, unsigned long nargs)
00087 {
00088         char **args = ptr;
00089         char progname[128];
00090         int result;
00091 
00092         KASSERT(nargs >= 1);
00093 
00094         /* Uncomment ONLY if you don't support passing argument */
00095         /*if (nargs > 2) {
00096                 kprintf("Warning: argument passing from menu not supported\n");
00097         }*/
00098 
00099         /* Hope we fit. */
00100         KASSERT(strlen(args[0]) < sizeof(progname));
00101 
00102         strcpy(progname, args[0]);
00103 
00104         result = runprogram(progname, (int)nargs, args);
00105 
00106         if (result) {
00107                 kprintf("Running program %s failed: %s\n", args[0],
00108                         strerror(result));
00109                 return;
00110         }
00111 
00112         /* NOTREACHED: runprogram only returns on error. */
00113 }
00114 
00115 /*
00116  * Common code for cmd_prog and cmd_shell.
00117  *
00118  * Note that this does not wait for the subprogram to finish, but
00119  * returns immediately to the menu. This is usually not what you want,
00120  * so you should have it call your system-calls-assignment waitpid
00121  * code after forking.
00122  *
00123  * Also note that because the subprogram's thread uses the "args"
00124  * array and strings, until you do this a race condition exists
00125  * between that code and the menu input code.
00126  */
00127 static
00128 int
00129 common_prog(int nargs, char **args)
00130 {
00131         int result;
00132 
00133 #if OPT_SYNCHPROBS
00134         kprintf("Warning: this probably won't work with a "
00135                 "synchronization-problems kernel.\n");
00136 #endif
00137 
00138         result = thread_fork(args[0] /* thread name */,
00139                         cmd_progthread /* thread function */,
00140                         args /* thread arg */, nargs /* thread arg */,
00141                         NULL);
00142         if (result) {
00143                 kprintf("thread_fork failed: %s\n", strerror(result));
00144                 return result;
00145         }
00146 
00147         return 0;
00148 }
00149 
00150 /*
00151  * Command for running an arbitrary userlevel program.
00152  */
00153 static
00154 int
00155 cmd_prog(int nargs, char **args)
00156 {
00157 
00158         if (nargs < 2) {
00159                 kprintf("Usage: p program [arguments]\n");
00160                 return EINVAL;
00161         }
00162 
00163         /* drop the leading "p" */
00164         args++;
00165         nargs--;
00166 
00167         return common_prog(nargs, args);
00168 }
00169 
00170 /*
00171  * Command for starting the system shell.
00172  */
00173 static
00174 int
00175 cmd_shell(int nargs, char **args)
00176 {
00177         (void)args;
00178         if (nargs != 1) {
00179                 kprintf("Usage: s\n");
00180                 return EINVAL;
00181         }
00182 
00183         args[0] = (char *)_PATH_SHELL;
00184 
00185         return common_prog(nargs, args);
00186 }
00187 
00188 /*
00189  * Command for changing directory.
00190  */
00191 static
00192 int
00193 cmd_chdir(int nargs, char **args)
00194 {
00195         if (nargs != 2) {
00196                 kprintf("Usage: cd directory\n");
00197                 return EINVAL;
00198         }
00199 
00200         return vfs_chdir(args[1]);
00201 }
00202 
00203 /*
00204  * Command for printing the current directory.
00205  */
00206 static
00207 int
00208 cmd_pwd(int nargs, char **args)
00209 {
00210         char buf[PATH_MAX+1];
00211         int result;
00212         struct iovec iov;
00213         struct uio ku;
00214 
00215         (void)nargs;
00216         (void)args;
00217 
00218         uio_kinit(&iov, &ku, buf, sizeof(buf)-1, 0, UIO_READ);
00219         result = vfs_getcwd(&ku);
00220         if (result) {
00221                 kprintf("vfs_getcwd failed (%s)\n", strerror(result));
00222                 return result;
00223         }
00224 
00225         /* null terminate */
00226         buf[sizeof(buf)-1-ku.uio_resid] = 0;
00227 
00228         /* print it */
00229         kprintf("%s\n", buf);
00230 
00231         return 0;
00232 }
00233 
00234 /*
00235  * Command for running sync.
00236  */
00237 static
00238 int
00239 cmd_sync(int nargs, char **args)
00240 {
00241         (void)nargs;
00242         (void)args;
00243 
00244         vfs_sync();
00245 
00246         return 0;
00247 }
00248 
00249 /*
00250  * Command for doing an intentional panic.
00251  */
00252 static
00253 int
00254 cmd_panic(int nargs, char **args)
00255 {
00256         (void)nargs;
00257         (void)args;
00258 
00259         panic("User requested panic\n");
00260         return 0;
00261 }
00262 
00263 /*
00264  * Command for shutting down.
00265  */
00266 static
00267 int
00268 cmd_quit(int nargs, char **args)
00269 {
00270         (void)nargs;
00271         (void)args;
00272 
00273         vfs_sync();
00274         sys_reboot(RB_POWEROFF);
00275         thread_exit();
00276         return 0;
00277 }
00278 
00279 /*
00280  * Command for mounting a filesystem.
00281  */
00282 
00283 /* Table of mountable filesystem types. */
00284 static const struct {
00285         const char *name;
00286         int (*func)(const char *device);
00287 } mounttable[] = {
00288 #if OPT_SFS
00289         { "sfs", sfs_mount },
00290 #endif
00291         { NULL, NULL }
00292 };
00293 
00294 static
00295 int
00296 cmd_mount(int nargs, char **args)
00297 {
00298         char *fstype;
00299         char *device;
00300         int i;
00301 
00302         if (nargs != 3) {
00303                 kprintf("Usage: mount fstype device:\n");
00304                 return EINVAL;
00305         }
00306 
00307         fstype = args[1];
00308         device = args[2];
00309 
00310         /* Allow (but do not require) colon after device name */
00311         if (device[strlen(device)-1]==':') {
00312                 device[strlen(device)-1] = 0;
00313         }
00314 
00315         for (i=0; mounttable[i].name; i++) {
00316                 if (!strcmp(mounttable[i].name, fstype)) {
00317                         return mounttable[i].func(device);
00318                 }
00319         }
00320         kprintf("Unknown filesystem type %s\n", fstype);
00321         return EINVAL;
00322 }
00323 
00324 static
00325 int
00326 cmd_unmount(int nargs, char **args)
00327 {
00328         char *device;
00329 
00330         if (nargs != 2) {
00331                 kprintf("Usage: unmount device:\n");
00332                 return EINVAL;
00333         }
00334 
00335         device = args[1];
00336 
00337         /* Allow (but do not require) colon after device name */
00338         if (device[strlen(device)-1]==':') {
00339                 device[strlen(device)-1] = 0;
00340         }
00341 
00342         return vfs_unmount(device);
00343 }
00344 
00345 /*
00346  * Command to set the "boot fs". 
00347  *
00348  * The boot filesystem is the one that pathnames like /bin/sh with
00349  * leading slashes refer to.
00350  *
00351  * The default bootfs is "emu0".
00352  */
00353 static
00354 int
00355 cmd_bootfs(int nargs, char **args)
00356 {
00357         char *device;
00358 
00359         if (nargs != 2) {
00360                 kprintf("Usage: bootfs device\n");
00361                 return EINVAL;
00362         }
00363 
00364         device = args[1];
00365 
00366         /* Allow (but do not require) colon after device name */
00367         if (device[strlen(device)-1]==':') {
00368                 device[strlen(device)-1] = 0;
00369         }
00370 
00371         return vfs_setbootfs(device);
00372 }
00373 
00374 static
00375 int
00376 cmd_kheapstats(int nargs, char **args)
00377 {
00378         (void)nargs;
00379         (void)args;
00380 
00381         kheap_printstats();
00382         
00383         return 0;
00384 }
00385 
00387 //
00388 // Menus.
00389 
00390 static
00391 void
00392 showmenu(const char *name, const char *x[])
00393 {
00394         int ct, half, i;
00395 
00396         kprintf("\n");
00397         kprintf("%s\n", name);
00398         
00399         for (i=ct=0; x[i]; i++) {
00400                 ct++;
00401         }
00402         half = (ct+1)/2;
00403 
00404         for (i=0; i<half; i++) {
00405                 kprintf("    %-36s", x[i]);
00406                 if (i+half < ct) {
00407                         kprintf("%s", x[i+half]);
00408                 }
00409                 kprintf("\n");
00410         }
00411 
00412         kprintf("\n");
00413 }
00414 
00415 static const char *opsmenu[] = {
00416         "[s]       Shell                     ",
00417         "[p]       Other program             ",
00418         "[mount]   Mount a filesystem        ",
00419         "[unmount] Unmount a filesystem      ",
00420         "[bootfs]  Set \"boot\" filesystem     ",
00421         "[pf]      Print a file              ",
00422         "[cd]      Change directory          ",
00423         "[pwd]     Print current directory   ",
00424         "[sync]    Sync filesystems          ",
00425         "[panic]   Intentional panic         ",
00426         "[q]       Quit and shut down        ",
00427         NULL
00428 };
00429 
00430 static
00431 int
00432 cmd_opsmenu(int n, char **a)
00433 {
00434         (void)n;
00435         (void)a;
00436 
00437         showmenu("OS/161 operations menu", opsmenu);
00438         return 0;
00439 }
00440 
00441 static const char *testmenu[] = {
00442         "[at]  Array test                    ",
00443         "[bt]  Bitmap test                   ",
00444         "[km1] Kernel malloc test            ",
00445         "[km2] kmalloc stress test           ",
00446         "[tt1] Thread test 1                 ",
00447         "[tt2] Thread test 2                 ",
00448         "[tt3] Thread test 3                 ",
00449 #if OPT_NET
00450         "[net] Network test                  ",
00451 #endif
00452         "[sy1] Semaphore test                ",
00453         "[sy2] Lock test             (1)     ",
00454         "[sy3] CV test               (1)     ",
00455         "[fs1] Filesystem test               ",
00456         "[fs2] FS read stress        (4)     ",
00457         "[fs3] FS write stress       (4)     ",
00458         "[fs4] FS write stress 2     (4)     ",
00459         "[fs5] FS create stress      (4)     ",
00460         NULL
00461 };
00462 
00463 static
00464 int
00465 cmd_testmenu(int n, char **a)
00466 {
00467         (void)n;
00468         (void)a;
00469 
00470         showmenu("OS/161 tests menu", testmenu);
00471         kprintf("    (1) These tests will fail until you finish the "
00472                 "synch assignment.\n");
00473         kprintf("    (4) These tests may fail until you finish the "
00474                 "file system assignment.\n");
00475         kprintf("\n");
00476 
00477         return 0;
00478 }
00479 
00480 static const char *mainmenu[] = {
00481         "[?o] Operations menu                ",
00482         "[?t] Tests menu                     ",
00483 #if OPT_SYNCHPROBS
00484         "[sp1] Whale Mating                  ",
00485 #if 0
00486         "[sp2] Cat/mouse                     ",
00487         "[sp3] Stoplight                     ",
00488 #endif
00489 #endif
00490         "[kh] Kernel heap stats              ",
00491         "[q] Quit and shut down              ",
00492         NULL
00493 };
00494 
00495 static
00496 int
00497 cmd_mainmenu(int n, char **a)
00498 {
00499         (void)n;
00500         (void)a;
00501 
00502         showmenu("OS/161 kernel menu", mainmenu);
00503         return 0;
00504 }
00505 
00507 //
00508 // Command table.
00509 
00510 static struct {
00511         const char *name;
00512         int (*func)(int nargs, char **args);
00513 } cmdtable[] = {
00514         /* menus */
00515         { "?",          cmd_mainmenu },
00516         { "h",          cmd_mainmenu },
00517         { "help",       cmd_mainmenu },
00518         { "?o",         cmd_opsmenu },
00519         { "?t",         cmd_testmenu },
00520 
00521         /* operations */
00522         { "s",          cmd_shell },
00523         { "p",          cmd_prog },
00524         { "mount",      cmd_mount },
00525         { "unmount",    cmd_unmount },
00526         { "bootfs",     cmd_bootfs },
00527         { "pf",         printfile },
00528         { "cd",         cmd_chdir },
00529         { "pwd",        cmd_pwd },
00530         { "sync",       cmd_sync },
00531         { "panic",      cmd_panic },
00532         { "q",          cmd_quit },
00533         { "exit",       cmd_quit },
00534         { "halt",       cmd_quit },
00535 
00536 #if OPT_SYNCHPROBS
00537         /* in-kernel synchronization problem(s) */
00538         { "sp1",        whalemating },
00539 #if 0
00540         { "sp2",        catmouse },
00541         { "sp3",        stoplight },
00542 #endif
00543 #endif
00544 
00545         /* stats */
00546         { "kh",         cmd_kheapstats },
00547 
00548         /* base system tests */
00549         { "at",         arraytest },
00550         { "bt",         bitmaptest },
00551         { "km1",        malloctest },
00552         { "km2",        mallocstress },
00553 #if OPT_NET
00554         { "net",        nettest },
00555 #endif
00556         { "tt1",        threadtest },
00557         { "tt2",        threadtest2 },
00558         { "tt3",        threadtest3 },
00559         { "sy1",        semtest },
00560 
00561         /* synchronization assignment tests */
00562         { "sy2",        locktest },
00563         { "sy3",        cvtest },
00564 
00565         /* file system assignment tests */
00566         { "fs1",        fstest },
00567         { "fs2",        readstress },
00568         { "fs3",        writestress },
00569         { "fs4",        writestress2 },
00570         { "fs5",        createstress },
00571 
00572         { NULL, NULL }
00573 };
00574 
00575 /*
00576  * Process a single command.
00577  */
00578 static
00579 int
00580 cmd_dispatch(char *cmd)
00581 {
00582         time_t beforesecs, aftersecs, secs;
00583         uint32_t beforensecs, afternsecs, nsecs;
00584         char *args[MAXMENUARGS];
00585         int nargs=0;
00586         char *word;
00587         char *context;
00588         int i, result;
00589 
00590         for (word = strtok_r(cmd, " \t", &context);
00591              word != NULL;
00592              word = strtok_r(NULL, " \t", &context)) {
00593 
00594                 if (nargs >= MAXMENUARGS) {
00595                         kprintf("Command line has too many words\n");
00596                         return E2BIG;
00597                 }
00598                 args[nargs++] = word;
00599         }
00600 
00601         if (nargs==0) {
00602                 return 0;
00603         }
00604 
00605         for (i=0; cmdtable[i].name; i++) {
00606                 if (*cmdtable[i].name && !strcmp(args[0], cmdtable[i].name)) {
00607                         KASSERT(cmdtable[i].func!=NULL);
00608 
00609                         gettime(&beforesecs, &beforensecs);
00610 
00611                         result = cmdtable[i].func(nargs, args);
00612 
00613                         gettime(&aftersecs, &afternsecs);
00614                         getinterval(beforesecs, beforensecs,
00615                                     aftersecs, afternsecs,
00616                                     &secs, &nsecs);
00617 
00618                         kprintf("Operation took %lu.%09lu seconds\n",
00619                                 (unsigned long) secs,
00620                                 (unsigned long) nsecs);
00621 
00622                         return result;
00623                 }
00624         }
00625 
00626         kprintf("%s: Command not found\n", args[0]);
00627         return EINVAL;
00628 }
00629 
00630 /*
00631  * Evaluate a command line that may contain multiple semicolon-delimited
00632  * commands.
00633  *
00634  * If "isargs" is set, we're doing command-line processing; print the
00635  * comamnds as we execute them and panic if the command is invalid or fails.
00636  */
00637 static
00638 void
00639 menu_execute(char *line, int isargs)
00640 {
00641         char *command;
00642         char *context;
00643         int result;
00644 
00645         for (command = strtok_r(line, ";", &context);
00646              command != NULL;
00647              command = strtok_r(NULL, ";", &context)) {
00648 
00649                 if (isargs) {
00650                         kprintf("OS/161 kernel: %s\n", command);
00651                 }
00652 
00653                 result = cmd_dispatch(command);
00654                 if (result) {
00655                         kprintf("Menu command failed: %s\n", strerror(result));
00656                         if (isargs) {
00657                                 panic("Failure processing kernel arguments\n");
00658                         }
00659                 }
00660         }
00661 }
00662 
00663 /*
00664  * Command menu main loop.
00665  *
00666  * First, handle arguments passed on the kernel's command line from
00667  * the bootloader. Then loop prompting for commands.
00668  *
00669  * The line passed in from the bootloader is treated as if it had been
00670  * typed at the prompt. Semicolons separate commands; spaces and tabs
00671  * separate words (command names and arguments).
00672  *
00673  * So, for instance, to mount an SFS on lhd0 and make it the boot
00674  * filesystem, and then boot directly into the shell, one would use
00675  * the kernel command line
00676  *
00677  *      "mount sfs lhd0; bootfs lhd0; s"
00678  */
00679 
00680 void
00681 menu(char *args)
00682 {
00683         char buf[64];
00684 
00685         menu_execute(args, 1);
00686 
00687         while (1) {
00688                 kprintf("OS/161 kernel [? for menu]: ");
00689                 kgets(buf, sizeof(buf));
00690                 menu_execute(buf, 0);
00691         }
00692 }
Generated on Sun Jun 27 22:12:13 2010 for Os/161 by  1.6.1
